sections.paragraph:
fut. יִפְצַר pr. (see Schult. Opp. Min. p. 168) to beat, to make blunt, i.q. Arab. فطر (whence פְּצִירָה, which see); hence to urge, to press, followed by בְּ of pers.-(a) with prayers (compare obtundere precibus), Gen 19:3 33:11 .-(b) with a hostile mind, Gen 19:9. Compare cognate פָּרַץ בְּ.

Hiphil, to strike on the mind; hence to be dull, stubborn, 1Sa 15:23. Inf. used as a noun, הַפְצַר stubbornness, ibid., coupled with מְרִי.

Derivative, פְּצִירָה.
